observations.summary: Phantom halts recur for a 3rd consecutive story (4.1, 4.2, 4.3): prepare-tests and implement-story both fired halts with outcomeHistory ["COMPLETE"] and no question in any responseExcerpt; traces are still not enabled, preventing root cause determination.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for prepare-tests (halts: 1, outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"], responseExcerpt is a normal ATDD scaffolding summary with no question) and implement-story (halts: 1, outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"], responseExcerpt is a normal implementation summary with no question). runner-errors.jsonl line 5: prepare-tests rc:124 timeout at 2026-07-12T10:16:04Z (salvagedOutput: yes) — the prepare-tests halt is correlated with the timeout. No trace directory exists at _bmad-output/pipeline/traces/4.3-a1-20260712T082910/ — trace-view reports 'no trace for this step' for all steps. Ledger entry 70 (4.1) hypothesized machinery artifact; ledger entry 75 (4.2) confirmed recurrence; both noted 'Enabling trace recording for the next run would allow widening into the mid-stream conversation' — traces remain unenabled for a 3rd run. Trends show haltsPerStory: 4.1=3, 4.2=1, 4.3=2.

observations.summary: prepare-tests opencode runner timed out (rc:124) with salvaged output for a 4th time (3.9 review-code, 3.11 unit-tests, 4.2 prepare-tests, 4.3 prepare-tests); the 4.5h duration is 10x the step average and is the maxMs in trends.
observations.evidence: runner-errors.jsonl line 5: {"ts":"2026-07-12T10:16:04Z","source":"opencode-runner","context":"Prepare tests (4.3) / initial run","rc":124,"salvagedOutput":"yes","stderrTail":""}. Journal step_end for prepare-tests (status: success, durationMs: 16423136 — the maxMs in trends, 10x the 1639859ms average; halts: 1; outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"]; responseExcerpt is a complete coherent summary: 'ATDD red-phase scaffolding complete for Story 4.3... Verification: typecheck passes, all 30 new tests are skipped (red phase), existing 303 tests still pass.'). The timeout fired at 10:16 (1.5h after step_start at 08:46) but step_end is at 13:19 (3h after the timeout), suggesting the step continued or was retried after the salvaged output. Ledger entries 41 (3.9), 56 (3.11), 76 (4.2) record prior occurrences.

observations.summary: Stale transitional markers persist for an 8th+ story; the 4.2 update_step to implement-story (expanding scope to ATDD checklists) did not work on its first run — the ATDD checklist was still stale with RED phase claims when review-tests ran, and implement-story left GREEN phase markers the prompt language does not cover.
observations.evidence: _bmad-output/test-artifacts/test-review-validation-report-4-3.md:110-129 — ATDD checklist had extensive stale claims fixed by review-tests: section header 'Red-Phase Test Scaffolds Created' → 'Test Scaffolds (All Active)', per-test status 'Status: RED — file does not exist yet' → 'Status: GREEN', implementation checklist items 'Test passes (green phase)' → 'Test passes', RED Phase verification 'marked with test.skip()' → 'test.skip() markers have been removed (all tests active)'. _bmad-output/test-artifacts/test-review-validation-report-4-3.md:81-83 — 3 Story 4.3 test files had 'Tests active — ... (GREEN).' header comments removed by review-tests (dockerfile.spec.ts:13, dockerignore.spec.ts:14, railway-project-structure.integration.spec.ts:11). Journal step_end for implement-story responseExcerpt does not mention updating the ATDD checklist or removing GREEN phase markers. Playbook implement-story prompt (line 66, updatedAt: 2026-07-12T08:29:09.203Z, updatedByRun: 4.2-a1-20260712T054504) says 'update any test-file headers and test planning artifacts (such as ATDD checklists) that claim tests are skipped, disabled, or in a red-phase state to reflect that the tests are now active' — the language 'skipped, disabled, or in a red-phase state' does not cover GREEN phase markers, and 'to reflect that the tests are now active' encourages relabeling to GREEN rather than removing the marker entirely. Ledger entries 23 (3.4), 32 (3.7), 36 (3.8), 42 (3.9), 51 (3.10), 58 (3.11), 64 (3.12), 71 (4.1), 77 (4.2) record prior occurrences; ledger entry 81 (4.2) records the applied update_step expanding scope to ATDD checklists. The 4.2 amendment was active for this run (run started 2026-07-12T08:29:11.494Z, 2 seconds after the amendment was applied).

observations.summary: The review-tests step's file search scope is agent-determined and inconsistent across runs: 4.2 searched only *.spec.ts and *.test.ts, missing *.test.tsx files entirely; 4.3 extended to *.test.tsx and found 12 stale markers from Stories 1.3, 2.2, 2.5, 3.2, 3.4, 3.7 that had persisted through 7+ review-tests runs.
observations.evidence: _bmad-output/test-artifacts/test-review-validation-report-4-3.md:71 — 'The Story 4.2 validation only searched *.spec.ts and *.test.ts files — it missed *.test.tsx files entirely. This validation extended the search to include *.test.tsx, finding 12 additional stale markers in web component test files.' _bmad-output/test-artifacts/test-review-validation-report-4-3.md:85-102 — 12 *.test.tsx files with stale 'TDD GREEN PHASE' / 'GREEN PHASE' markers from Stories 1.3, 2.2, 2.5, 3.2, 3.4, 3.7 (SideNavigation, RepositoryUrlForm, CredentialErrorBanner, ArtifactCard, project-map/page, SlashCommandPicker, ArtifactLoadError, ConversationPane, ArtifactListEntry, artifacts/page, ToolPill, ArtifactViewer). These markers persisted through review-tests runs for Stories 3.8, 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 4.1, 4.2 — 7 runs where the search did not cover *.test.tsx files. Playbook review-tests prompt (line 110) says 'wherever found during the search, including those from earlier stories in the same directories' — 'in the same directories' is ambiguous and does not specify comprehensive directory or file-type coverage. The bmad-testarch-test-review skill (steps-c/step-02-discover-tests.md:42) supports suite scope ('glob all tests in repo') but the prompt does not activate it.

amendments.prompt: Develop story autonomously, completing every task listed in the story spec before finishing. For each task, follow red-green-refactor: (1) RED — un-skip the task's test scaffold (if one exists from prepare-tests), run it, and confirm it fails for the expected reason (e.g., endpoint not found, method not implemented); if it fails for an unexpected reason (import error, syntax error, broken setup), fix the test before implementing; if no scaffold exists, write a failing test and confirm it fails for the right reason; (2) GREEN — implement minimal code to make the test pass; (3) REFACTOR — improve code structure while keeping tests green, applying patterns from Dev Notes. After green, remove the test's skip marker and remove all transitional phase markers from test-file headers and test planning artifacts (such as ATDD checklists) — any comment, header, or status line that describes a TDD phase transition (whether claiming tests are skipped, disabled, in a red-phase, or in a green-phase state) must be removed entirely rather than relabeled, as these markers become stale immediately after the transition is complete; update story coverage lists in those headers to include the current story — the story is not complete while skipped tests or stale test-file headers remain for done tasks. Before marking the story complete, re-read project-context.md and verify each non-functional pattern documented there has been applied to your changes where applicable — apply any missing patterns before finishing, do not wait for the NFR review to catch them. For any decision that arises, consult @_bmad-output/decision-policy.md before escalating: if a rule covers it, decide and continue. HALT only for decisions no rule covers.
amendments.rationale: The current prompt language covers markers claiming tests are 'skipped, disabled, or in a red-phase state' but not green-phase markers, so implement-story relabels red-phase headers to green-phase rather than removing them, leaving transitional artifacts that review-tests must clean up every run.

amendments.prompt: run Validate mode. Search all test directories in the project and all test file types (including component test files), not just the story's own test files. Flag skipped story-related tests for un-skipping or removal (with reason). Fix stale transitional markers directly wherever found during the search, including those from earlier stories — comments/headers claiming tests are skipped/disabled/red-phase when they're actually active must be updated to reflect current state. Do not defer out-of-scope markers to a separate validation. Treat empty placeholder test stubs (active tests with no assertions, only a comment or empty body) as transitional artifacts — remove them directly wherever found during the search, including those from earlier stories, since an empty test that passes trivially inflates the count without verifying behavior.
amendments.rationale: The prompt's 'in the same directories' language does not specify comprehensive directory or file-type coverage, so the agent's search scope is inconsistent across runs — 4.2 missed component test files entirely, leaving 12 stale markers that persisted through 7+ runs before 4.3 caught them by chance.
